title: Adoption-channel outreach + candidate partner shortlist
context: The project's 'delivered, not merged' bar requires datasets to be adopted into real teaching before they count as shipped outcomes. No adoption partner has been secured yet (verifiedNeed:false on all tasks). To de-risk this, M0 includes explicit outreach to candidate adoption channels: Data Carpentry / The Carpentries, Bioconductor / Galaxy Training Network, university bioinformatics or biostatistics courses, cancer-data MOOCs, and patient-advocate research-literacy programs. Opening at least one outreach thread in M0 is an M0 exit criterion. This is a research/coordination task: identify, prioritize, and contact candidate channels.
objective: Open >= 1 adoption-channel outreach thread and produce a committed candidate-partner shortlist ranking realistic adoption channels by fit, audience size, and timeline.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one outreach thread opened with a named candidate adoption channel (e.g. Data Carpentry lesson proposal, Galaxy Training issue, university course contact) by the end of M0.

A committed shortlist document ranks >= 3 candidate channels by: audience fit (bioinformatics/biostatistics learners), realistic adoption timeline, lesson/dataset format requirements, and contact pathway.
Each candidate entry includes: channel name, URL, contact method, format requirements for contributed datasets/lessons, and estimated audience size.
Document distinguishes between self-serve channels (Zenodo + training-repo PR) and partner-confirmed channels requiring an adoption agreement.
Document committed to the project repo; commit DCO signed-off.
output: A committed adoption-channel outreach document with a ranked candidate-partner shortlist and at least one opened outreach thread, providing the steward a concrete starting point for securing adoption partners.
